title: PSETEX
description: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
`PSETEX` works exactly like [`SETEX`]() with the sole difference that the expire time is specified in milliseconds instead of seconds. ## Examples PSETEX mykey 1000 "Hello" PTTL mykey GET mykey ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Simple string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings): `OK`. **RESP3:** [Simple string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings): `OK`.
intro:
`PSETEX` works exactly like [`SETEX`]() with the sole difference that the expire
time is specified in milliseconds instead of seconds.

sections.title: Overview

`PSETEX` works exactly like [`SETEX`]() with the sole difference that the expire
time is specified in milliseconds instead of seconds.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
PSETEX mykey 1000 "Hello"
PTTL mykey
GET mykey
